title: F420-based Fluorescence-Activated Cell Sorting of Sedimentary Microbes
author: Jeemin Rhim

steps.title: Collect sediment samples from the environment (e
steps.instruction: Collect sediment samples from the environment (e.g., marine sediment core samples). Do not freeze or add fixatives to the samples. Store samples at 4ºC until cell extraction (next step).

steps.title: Add ~1 g of sediment to...
steps.instruction: Add ~1 g of sediment to a 15-mL conical bottom centrifuge tube.

steps.title: Add glycerol-TE to samples to a final concentration of ~5
steps.instruction: Add glycerol-TE to samples to a final concentration of ~5.5%.

steps.title: Immediately before sorting, thaw cryotubes containing samples at 42ºC
steps.instruction: Immediately before sorting, thaw cryotubes containing samples at 42ºC.

steps.title: Prepare a DNA contamination-free working environment
steps.instruction: Prepare a DNA contamination-free working environment: wipe down all working surfaces and pipettes with (in the following order) 70% ethanol, 10% bleach, and ultrapure water. Optionally, clean the surfaces and pipettes with DNA AWAY™ surface decontaminant at the end. Use new PCR-grade pipette tips. UV-crosslink all 2-mL tubes used for amplification for at least 30-60 minutes.

steps.title: Add 5 mL of sterile 3X...
steps.instruction: Add 5 mL of sterile 3X PBS to each 15-mL tube. Gently invert tubes enough to mix the sediment and PBS, resulting in sediment slurries.

steps.title: Place the 15-mL tubes containing sediment...
steps.instruction: Place the 15-mL tubes containing sediment slurries in a rack in an ice bath. Ultrasonicate for 2 x 20-second cycles at 7-10 Watts.

steps.title: Top off each 15-mL tube with...
steps.instruction: Top off each 15-mL tube with additional 5 mL of 3X PBS. The slurries should contain 10 mL of 3X PBS and ~1 g of sediment at this point.

steps.title: Vortex the slurries for 10 seconds
steps.instruction: Vortex the slurries for 10 seconds.

steps.title: Allow them to settle on ice for 10-15 minutes
steps.instruction: Allow them to settle on ice for 10-15 minutes.

steps.title: For each sample, aliquot 750 µL...
steps.instruction: For each sample, aliquot 750 µL of the supernatant into each of four replicate 2-mL microcentrifuge tubes.

steps.title: Using a 22G needle and a...
steps.instruction: Using a 22G needle and a 1-mL syringe, add 750 µL of 60% (w/v) Nycodenz to the bottom of each tube. As long as the tip of the needle releases Nycodenz at the bottom of the tube, the dense Nycodenz layer will stay below the sample.

steps.title: Centrifuge the 2-mL tubes containing sample...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the 2-mL tubes containing sample and Nycodenz at 14,000 g for 20 minutes at 4ºC.

steps.title: Using a pipette, carefully collect the...
steps.instruction: Using a pipette, carefully collect the sample supernatant and the "cell layer" at the interface between the supernatant and the Nycodenz layer. Combine the supernatant and cell layers from two replicate tubes into one 2-mL tube. This will result in two 2-mL tubes per sample, each containing >1.4 mL of sample.

steps.title: Optional
steps.instruction: Optional: the supernatant and cell layer collected from Step 11 may vary slightly across samples. You may transfer an exact amount of sample into a new 2-mL cryotube.

steps.title: Mix the cryotubes gently by inverting three times
steps.instruction: Mix the cryotubes gently by inverting three times. Allow to sit at room temperature for 15-20 minutes. It is important to incubate the samples at room temperature prior to freezing. Shorter incubation times (1-5 minutes) will result in decreased cell recovery.

steps.title: After the 15-20 minute incubation, flash-freeze...
steps.instruction: After the 15-20 minute incubation, flash-freeze the cryotubes in liquid nitrogen.

steps.title: Keep the flash-frozen cryovials at –80ºC...
steps.instruction: Keep the flash-frozen cryovials at –80ºC until cell sorting (next step).

steps.title: Perform cytometric measurements of forward scatter...
steps.instruction: Perform cytometric measurements of forward scatter (FSC), side scatter (SSC), and the F 420 fluorescence as described in Lambrecht et al. (2017). Briefly, the 488 nm laser is used for FSC (488/10) and SSC (trigger signal, 488/10), and the 405 nm laser is used for the F 420 fluorescence. Flow cytometric quantification, sorting and sequencing of methanogenic archaea based on F(420) autofluorescence..

steps.title: Sort F 420 -positive particles into...
steps.instruction: Sort F 420 -positive particles into a new (PCR-clean) 2-mL tube. Note: you will need up to 3 µL per 12-µL reaction for the whole genome amplification protocol described below; sort enough particles for replicate reactions. If planning for single-cell sequencing, sort single F420-positive particles into a 96-well or 384-well plate.

steps.title: Clean the sorting environment
steps.instruction: Clean the sorting environment: wipe down the inside surfaces of the sorting chamber and other working surfaces with (in the following order) 70% ethanol, 10% bleach, and ultrapure water. Optionally, clean the surfaces with DNA AWAY™ surface decontaminant at the end.

steps.title: Keep sorted samples at –80ºC until genome amplification (next step)
steps.instruction: Keep sorted samples at –80ºC until genome amplification (next step).

steps.title: Immediately before amplification, thaw sorted samples at 42ºC
steps.instruction: Immediately before amplification, thaw sorted samples at 42ºC.

steps.title: Conduct primary template-directed amplification (PTA) DNA...
steps.instruction: Conduct primary template-directed amplification (PTA) DNA amplification on samples following the ResolveDNA® Whole Genome Amplification Kit Protocol. Below are modifications we recommend for environmental samples containing bacteria and archaea.

steps.title: Conduct 5 freeze-thaw cycles on samples...
steps.instruction: Conduct 5 freeze-thaw cycles on samples prior to the lysis step of the protocol. This can be done by aliquoting just the samples in a PCR plate first, performing the freeze-thaw cycles, then finish preparing the plate with negative and positive controls before proceeding with the ResolveDNA® Whole Genome Amplification Kit Protocol.

steps.title: The thermal cycler program for DNA...
steps.instruction: The thermal cycler program for DNA amplification in the Kit Protocol includes a hold at 30ºC for 2.5 hours. For sorted environmental samples (e.g., microbial cells extracted from sediments and sorted as described above), we increased the duration of this isothermal amplification to ~4 hours. It is important to include at least one (preferably two or more) negative control to ensure that the prolonged amplification did not result in amplification of negative controls. To do this, we added 0.12 µL of SYTO™ Green Fluorescent Nucleic Acid Stain (SYTO 9) to the Reaction Mix (final concentration of 5 µM) and monitored fluorescence of samples and controls on a real-time PCR machine. The 30ºC hold was terminated at the first sign of fluorescence increase in negative controls.

steps.title: Amplified products are stored at –20ºC...
steps.instruction: Amplified products are stored at –20ºC (or –80ºC for long-term storage). Samples are ready for downstream sequencing applications.

steps.title: Alternatively, whole genome amplification can be...
steps.instruction: Alternatively, whole genome amplification can be done using a different kit (e.g., QIAGEN® REPLI-g® Single Cell Kit).

steps.title: The cell extraction protocol was adapted from Lloyd et al
steps.instruction: The cell extraction protocol was adapted from Lloyd et al. (2013) and Fazi et al. (2005). Predominant archaea in marine sediments degrade detrital proteins.. Bacterial communities associated with benthic organic matter in headwater stream microhabitats..
